debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
f : U → V
g : V → U
h : g ∘ f = id
⊢ image g ∘ image f = id

proof:
by
  funext s
  have h₁ : (image g) (image f s) = s := by
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    have h₂ : x ∈ (image g) (image f s) ↔ x ∈ s := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ (image g) (image f s), then x ∈ s
        intro hx
        rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
        have h₃ : y ∈ image f s := hy
        rcases h₃ with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
        have h₄ : g (f z) = z := by
          have h₅ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
            rw [h₅]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₆
        simp_all
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ (image g) (image f s)
        intro hx
        have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := by
          exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
        have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by
          have h₅ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            rw [h₅]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₆
        exact ⟨f x, h₃, by simp [h₄]⟩
    simp_all
  simp_all
failed_attempts:
by
          have h₅ : (n : Int) % 2 = 0 := by simpa using h₃
          have h₆ : (n : Int) = 2 * (n / 2) := by
            omega
          exact h₆
        linarith
      simp_all [h₂]
      <;>
      ring_nf at * <;>
      omega
    · -- Prove that the composition of the inverse functions is the identity
      intro n
      have h₂ : (2 * (n : Int)) / 2 = n := by
        omega
      simp_all [h₂]
      <;>
      ring_nf at * <;>
      omega
  -- Since the set is equivalent to the integers, it is denumerable
  have h₂ : denum {n : Int | n % 2 = 0} := by
    apply denum.congr h₁
    exact denum_int
  exact h₂

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext S
    have h₂ : (image g ∘ image f) S = S := by
      calc
        (image g ∘ image f) S = image g (image f S) := rfl
        _ = S := by
          have h₃ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₄ : ∀ a : U, g (f a) = a := by
            intro a
            have h₅ : (g ∘ f) a = id a := by rw [h]
            simp [Function.comp_apply, Function.id] at h₅ ⊢
            exact h₅
          have h₅ : image g (image f S) = S := by
            apply Set.ext
            intro x
            simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            constructor
            · intro ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
              obtain ⟨z, hz, hy'⟩ := hy
              have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₄ z
              have h₇ : g (f z) = y := by simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
              have h₈ : y = z := by simp_all
              simp_all
            · intro hx
              have h₆ : g (f x) = x := h₄ x
              refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩, _⟩
              simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          simp_all
    simp_all
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g (image f s)) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    have h₂ : x ∈ (image g (image f s)) ↔ x ∈ s := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ image g (image f s), then x ∈ s
        intro hx
        -- There exists y ∈ image f s such that g y = x
        rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
        -- There exists z ∈ s such that f z = y
        rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
        -- Now we have g (f z) = x, but g ∘ f = id, so z = x
        have h₃ : g (f z) = z := by
          have h₄ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₄
        -- Therefore, x = z ∈ s
        simp_all
      · -- Prove the backward direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ image g (image f s)
        intro hx
        -- Since x ∈ s, f x ∈ image f s
        have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := by
          exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
        -- And g (f x) = x
        have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by
          have h₅ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₅
        -- Therefore, x ∈ image g (image f s)
        exact ⟨f x, h₃, by simp [h₄]⟩
    -- Use h₂ to prove the set equality
    simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
  -- Now use h₁ to prove the main statement
  funext s
  rw [h₁]

by
  funext s
  have h₁ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
      rw [h]
      <;> simp
    exact h₂
  have h₂ : image g (image f s) = s := by
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in g(image f s), then x is in s.
      intro h₃
      obtain ⟨y, hy₁, hy₂⟩ := h₃
      obtain ⟨z, hz₁, hz₂⟩ := hy₁
      use z
      <;> simp_all [h₁]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in s, then x is in g(image f s).
      intro h₃
      use f x
      constructor
      · -- Show that f x is in image f s.
        use x
        <;> simp_all
      · -- Show that g(f x) = x.
        have h₄ := h₁ x
        simp_all
  simp_all

by
  funext s
  have h₁ : (g ∘ f) = id := h
  have h₂ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₃ := congr_fun h₁ x
    simpa using h₃
  ext x
  simp only [Function.comp_apply, image_image]
  have h₃ : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ s → x ∈ s := by
    intro x hx
    exact hx
  have h₄ : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ s → g (f x) = x := by
    intro x hx
    exact h₂ x
  -- Use the fact that g ∘ f = id to show that the image of the image is the original set
  simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
  <;>
  (try aesop) <;>
  (try
    {
      intros
      <;>
      (try
        {
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Set.mem_image]
          <;>
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          aesop
        })
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      simp_all [Set.mem_image]
      <;>
      aesop
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      simp_all [Set.mem_image]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g (image f s)) = s := by
    intro s
    have h₂ : g ∘ f = id := h
    have h₃ : ∀ x : U, (g ∘ f) x = x := by
      intro x
      rw [h₂]
      <;> simp
    have h₄ : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
      intro x
      have h₅ := h₃ x
      simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₅ ⊢
      exact h₅
    have h₅ : image g (image f s) = s := by
      apply Set.Subset.antisymm
      · -- Prove that image g (image f s) ⊆ s
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_image, exists_prop] at hx ⊢
        rcases hx with ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
        have h₆ : g (f z) = z := h₄ z
        simp_all [h₆]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove that s ⊆ image g (image f s)
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_image, exists_prop]
        have h₆ : g (f x) = x := h₄ x
        refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩, _⟩
        simp [h₆]
    exact h₅
  funext s
  have h₂ := h₁ s
  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
  <;> aesop

by
  ext s
  have h₁ : g ∘ f = id := h
  have h₂ : Set.image g (Set.image f s) = s := by
    calc
      Set.image g (Set.image f s) = Set.image (g ∘ f) s := by rw [Set.image_comp]
      _ = Set.image (id : U → U) s := by congr 1 <;> ext x <;> simp [h₁]
      _ = s := by simp
  simpa using h₂

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
      rw [h]
      <;> simp
    exact h₂
  funext s
  have h₂ : (image g) ((image f) s) = s := by
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in (image g) ((image f) s), then x is in s
      rintro ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
      -- Since g (f z) = z, we can show that x is in s
      have h₃ : g (f z) = z := h₁ z
      have h₄ : g y = z := by
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      have h₅ : y = f z := by
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      have h₆ : z ∈ s := by
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in s, then x is in (image g) ((image f) s)
      intro h₃
      -- Since g (f x) = x, we can find y = f x and z = g (f x) = x
      refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, h₃, rfl⟩, _⟩
      have h₄ : g (f x) = x := h₁ x
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]

by
  funext s
  have h₁ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
    intro x
    have h₂ : (g ∘ f) x = id x := by rw [h]
    simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂ ⊢
    exact h₂
  ext x
  simp only [Function.comp_apply, Set.mem_image]
  <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      constructor <;> intro h₂ <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try {
        rcases h₂ with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
        use x
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
      (try {
        rcases h₂ with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
        use g y
        <;> aesop
      })
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })

by
  have h1 : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) ((image f) s) = s := by
    intro s
    have h2 : ∀ (x : U), x ∈ (image g) ((image f) s) ↔ x ∈ s := by
      intro x
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Function.comp]
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ (image g)( (image f)(s) ), then x ∈ s
        intro h3
        rcases h3 with ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, hy⟩
        have h4 : g (f z) = z := by
          have h5 : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
            have h6 : (g ∘ f) = id := h
            simp [h6]
          simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h5
        simp_all [h4]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove the backward direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ (image g)( (image f)(s) )
        intro h3
        have h4 : f x ∈ Set.image f s := by
          apply Set.mem_image_of_mem
          exact h3
        refine' ⟨f x, h4, _⟩
        have h5 : g (f x) = x := by
          have h6 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
            have h7 : (g ∘ f) = id := h
            simp [h7]
          simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h6
        simp_all [h5]
        <;> aesop
    -- Use the membership condition to prove the set equality
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    exact h2 x
  -- Prove that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
  funext s
  apply h1

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext A
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    have h₂ : ( (image g) ∘ (image f) ) A = image g (image f A) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : image g (image f A) = A := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro y
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at *
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if y ∈ image g (image f A), then y ∈ A
        intro hy
        rcases hy with ⟨v, ⟨u, hu, huv⟩, hvy⟩
        have h₄ : g (f u) = u := by
          have h₅ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) u = u := by
            rw [h₅]
            <;> simp
          simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₆ ⊢
          <;> exact h₆
        have h₇ : g v = y := hvy
        have h₈ : f u = v := by
          have h₉ : f u = v := by
            linarith
          exact h₉
        have h₉ : g (f u) = g v := by rw [h₈]
        have h₁₀ : g v = y := hvy
        have h₁₁ : g (f u) = y := by rw [h₉, h₁₀]
        have h₁₂ : g (f u) = u := by
          have h₁₃ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₁₄ : (g ∘ f) u = u := by
            rw [h₁₃]
            <;> simp
          simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₁₄ ⊢
          <;> exact h₁₄
        have h₁₃ : u = y := by
          linarith
        have h₁₄ : y ∈ A := by
          simpa [h₁₃] using hu
        exact h₁₄
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if y ∈ A, then y ∈ image g (image f A)
        intro hy
        have h₄ : y ∈ A := hy
        have h₅ : f y ∈ (image f A) := by
          refine' ⟨y, h₄, _⟩
          <;> simp
        have h₆ : g (f y) = y := by
          have h₇ : g ∘ f = id := h
          have h₈ : (g ∘ f) y = y := by
            rw [h₇]
            <;> simp
          simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₈ ⊢
          <;> exact h₈
        refine' ⟨f y, h₅, _⟩
        <;> simp [h₆]
        <;>
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;>
        aesop
    rw [h₃]
    <;> simp
  exact h₁

by
  have h1 : ∀ (x : U), (image g) (image f {x}) = {x} := by
    intro x
    have h2 : image f {x} = {f x} := by
      ext y
      simp [image_singleton]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h2]
    have h3 : image g {f x} = {g (f x)} := by
      ext y
      simp [image_singleton]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : g (f x) = x := by
      have h5 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
        rw [h]
        <;> simp
      simpa using h5
    rw [h4]
    <;> simp [image_singleton]
  ext s
  have h2 : (image g ∘ image f) s = s := by
    classical
    have h3 : s = ⋃ (x : U), if x ∈ s then {x} else ∅ := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro x
      simp [Set.mem_iUnion]
      <;> aesop
    rw [h3]
    simp [Function.comp_apply, image_iUnion]
    apply iUnion_congr
    intro x _
    by_cases hx : x ∈ s <;> simp_all [h1]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h2]
  <;> simp

by omega
          have h₄ : n = 2 * (n / 2) := by omega
          omega
        })
      · -- Prove that f is injective
        intro ⟨n₁, hn₁⟩ ⟨n₂, hn₂⟩ h
        simp [f] at h
        -- Since n₁ and n₂ are even, n₁ / 2 = n₂ / 2 implies n₁ = n₂
        have h₃ : n₁ % 2 = 0 := by
          cases' hn₁ with k hk
          omega
        have h₄ : n₂ % 2 = 0 := by
          cases' hn₂ with k hk
          omega
        have h₅ : n₁ = n₂ := by
          have h₆ : n₁ / 2 = n₂ / 2 := by omega
          have h₇ : n₁ % 2 = 0 := h₃
          have h₈ : n₂ % 2 = 0 := h₄
          have h₉ : n₁ = 2 * (n₁ / 2) := by omega
          have h₁₀ : n₂ = 2 * (n₂ / 2) := by omega
          omega
        simp_all
    -- Use the bijection to show that the set of even integers is denumerable
    exact h₂.denumerable
  -- Conclude the proof
  exact h₁

by
  have h_main : ∀ (s : Set U), image g (image f s) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.ext
    intro x
    simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ image g (image f s), then x ∈ s
      rintro ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, hy⟩, hx⟩
      -- Since g ∘ f = id, we have g (f z) = z. But hy says f z = y, so g y = z.
      have h1 : g (f z) = z := by
        have h2 : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h2
      have h3 : g y = x := by simpa [hx] using hx
      have h4 : f z = y := by simpa [hy] using hy
      have h5 : g y = z := by
        calc
          g y = g (f z) := by rw [h4]
          _ = z := h1
      have h6 : x = z := by
        calc
          x = g y := by rw [h3]
          _ = z := h5
      rw [h6]
      exact hz
    · -- Prove the backward direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ image g (image f s)
      intro hx
      -- Since x ∈ s, we can take y = f x. Then y ∈ image f s and g y = x.
      have h1 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
        have h2 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h2
      have h2 : g (f x) = x := by
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h1
      refine' ⟨f x, ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩, _⟩
      -- Now we need to show that g (f x) = x, which follows from the hypothesis.
      exact h2
  
  have h_final : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext s
    rw [Function.comp_apply]
    rw [h_main s]
    <;> simp [Set.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  
  exact h_final

by
  have h1 : (image g ∘ image f) = id := by
    have h2 : ∀ (S : Set U), image g (image f S) = S := by
      intro S
      have h3 : ∀ x, x ∈ image g (image f S) ↔ x ∈ S := by
        intro x
        have h4 : x ∈ image g (image f S) ↔ ∃ y, y ∈ image f S ∧ g y = x := by
          simp [Set.mem_image]
          <;> tauto
        have h5 : (∃ y, y ∈ image f S ∧ g y = x) ↔ x ∈ S := by
          constructor
          · -- Prove the forward direction: if there exists a y, then x ∈ S
            intro ⟨y, hy, hyx⟩
            have h6 : y ∈ image f S := hy
            have h7 : ∃ z, z ∈ S ∧ f z = y := by
              simp_all [Set.mem_image]
              <;> tauto
            rcases h7 with ⟨z, hz, hz'⟩
            have h8 : g y = x := hyx
            have h9 : g (f z) = x := by
              calc
                g (f z) = g y := by rw [hz']
                _ = x := h8
            have h10 : g (f z) = z := by
              have h11 : g ∘ f = id := h
              have h12 : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
                rw [h11]
                <;> simp
              simp [Function.comp_apply] at h12 ⊢
              <;> exact h12
            have h11 : z = x := by
              calc
                z = g (f z) := by rw [h10]
                _ = x := by rw [h9]
            exact hz.trans h11
          · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ S, then there exists a y
            intro hx
            use f x
            constructor
            · -- Prove that f x ∈ image f S
              have h6 : f x ∈ image f S := by
                have h7 : x ∈ S := hx
                exact Set.mem_image_of_mem f h7
              exact h6
            · -- Prove that g (f x) = x
              have h7 : g (f x) = x := by
                have h8 : g ∘ f = id := h
                have h9 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
                  rw [h8]
                  <;> simp
                simp [Function.comp_apply] at h9 ⊢
                <;> exact h9
              exact h7
        have h6 : x ∈ image g (image f S) ↔ x ∈ S := by
          rw [h4]
          rw [h5]
        exact h6
      have h7 : image g (image f S) = S := by
        apply Set.ext
        intro x
        exact h3 x
      exact h7
    funext S
    have h3 : (image g ∘ image f) S = S := by
      have h4 : (image g ∘ image f) S = image g (image f S) := rfl
      rw [h4]
      have h5 : image g (image f S) = S := h2 S
      rw [h5]
    exact h3
  exact h1

by
  have h1 : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) ((image f) s) = s := by
    intro s
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove (image g) ((image f) s) ⊆ s
      intro x hx
      -- hx : x ∈ g '' (f '' s)
      -- So there exists y ∈ f '' s such that g y = x
      rcases hx with ⟨y, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
      -- z ∈ s, f z = y, g y = x
      have h2 : g (f z) = z := by
        have h3 : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        exact h3
      -- g (f z) = z and g (f z) = x, so z = x
      <;> simp_all [h2]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Prove s ⊆ (image g) ((image f) s)
      intro x hx
      -- hx : x ∈ s
      -- We need to find y ∈ f '' s such that g y = x
      have h2 : f x ∈ (image f) s := by

        refine' ⟨f x, h2, _⟩
        rw [h3]
      exact h4

  funext s
  exact h1 s

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by
    funext S
    -- Prove that image g (image f S) = S
    apply Set.Subset.antisymm
    · -- Prove image g (image f S) ⊆ S
      intro x hx
      -- x ∈ image g (image f S)
      -- Then there exists y ∈ image f S such that g(y) = x
      rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
      -- y ∈ image f S
      -- Then there exists z ∈ S such that f(z) = y
      rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
      -- Now x = g(f(z)) = z (since g ∘ f = id)
      have h₂ : g (f z) = z := by
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) z = z := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa using h₃
      -- Therefore x = z ∈ S
      exact hz
    · -- Prove S ⊆ image g (image f S)
      intro x hx
      -- x ∈ S
      -- Let y = f(x) ∈ image f S
      have h₂ : g (f x) = x := by
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        simpa using h₃
      -- Now g(y) = g(f(x)) = x
      -- Therefore x ∈ image g (image f S)
      have h₃ : x ∈ image g (image f S) := by
        have h₄ : f x ∈ image f S := by
          exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
        have h₅ : g (f x) ∈ image g (image f S) := by
          exact ⟨f x, h₄, rfl⟩
        have h₆ : g (f x) = x := by
          exact h₂
        rw [h₆] at h₅
        exact h₅
      exact h₃
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : (image g) ∘ (image f) = id := by

          simp (config := { contextual := true }) [exists_prop] <;>

          constructor

            refine' ⟨z, hz, _⟩
            rw [h₂]
            <;> simp_all
            <;> aesop
          · intro h₄
            obtain ⟨z, hz, hgz⟩ := h₄
            refine' ⟨f z, ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩, _⟩
            simp_all
            <;> aesop
        _ = {x | ∃ z ∈ s, (id z : U) = x} := by
          congr 1
          ext x

            refine' ⟨z, hz, _⟩
            simp_all
            <;> aesop

            have h₅ : g (f z) = z := by
              have h₆ : g (f z) = (g ∘ f) z := rfl

              have h₇ : (g ∘ f : U → U) = id := h₂
              rw [h₇]

            simp_all

          ext x

by
  have h₁ : ∀ (s : Set U), (image g) (image f s) = s := by
    intro s

      intro x
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if x ∈ (image g) (image f s), then x ∈ s
        intro hx
        rcases hx with ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
        rcases hy with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
        -- Since g ∘ f = id, we have g (f z) = z
        have h₃ : g (f z) = z := by
          have h₄ := congr_fun h z
          simp at h₄ ⊢
          <;> simp_all
        -- Therefore, y = z, and since z ∈ s, we have y ∈ s
        <;> simp_all
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if x ∈ s, then x ∈ (image g) (image f s)
        intro hx
        -- For x ∈ s, take y = f x. Then y ∈ image f s
        have h₃ : f x ∈ image f s := by
          exact ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
        -- Now, g (f x) = x because g ∘ f = id
        have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by
          have h₅ := congr_fun h x
          simp at h₅ ⊢
          <;> simp_all
        -- Therefore, x = g (f x) ∈ (image g) (image f s)
        exact ⟨f x, h₃, by simp [h₄]⟩
    -- Since the memberships are equivalent, the sets are equal
    ext x
    exact h₂ x
  -- Now, we show that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
  funext s
  have h₂ := h₁ s
  simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
  <;> aesop
